title: brachyactis ciliata subsp. laurentiana
intro: Brachyactis ciliata subsp. laurentiana, also known as Mountain Fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Mountain Fleabane include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Brachyactis ciliata subsp. laurentiana typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the leaves and flowers of the plant, which can be used as teas, salves, or administered orally.
